# 'gs anchor track' adopts an existing worktree's anchor branch into
# git-spice's anchor registry.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-29T14:00:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# A worktree created outside git-spice (plain 'git worktree add'),
# with its own local branch 'dev' acting as an anchor.
git worktree add $WORK/wt1 -b dev
cd $WORK/wt1
cp $WORK/feat1.txt feat1.txt

# Before tracking, git-spice does not know 'dev' is an anchor, so a branch
# based on it cannot be tracked.
git add feat1.txt
! gs bc feat1 -m 'Add feat1'

# Adopt the worktree's anchor.
gs anchor track
stderr 'Tracking dev as the anchor for worktree'

# Now a branch created in the worktree roots at the worktree anchor.
gs bc feat1 -m 'Add feat1'
gs ls
stderr 'feat1'
stderr 'dev'

# Re-tracking is idempotent.
gs bco dev
gs anchor track
stderr 'Tracking dev as the anchor for worktree'

# A worktree may have only one anchor: tracking a second, different
# branch as this worktree's anchor is refused.
git branch dev2
! gs anchor track --name dev2
stderr 'already has anchor'

# A tracked stack branch cannot be registered as an anchor.
! gs anchor track --name feat1
stderr 'must be a graph root'

# From the primary worktree on the canonical trunk, tracking is refused.
cd $WORK/repo
! gs anchor track
stderr 'canonical trunk'

-- repo/dummy.txt --
dummy
-- feat1.txt --
feat1 content
